name: Serper Google Search API
what_it_does: Provides real-time Google Search results via a low-latency REST API, returning structured SERP data including organic results, knowledge graph, news, images, shopping, and maps.
use_cases: Giving agents real-time Google web search capability for up-to-date information retrieval
Fetching structured Google News results for current events monitoring and summarization
Retrieving Google Shopping results for product comparison and price discovery agents
Augmenting RAG pipelines with fresh web data not present in a static vector store
Running multi-query research workflows that require Google's index coverage and freshness
not_for: Privacy-sensitive search contexts where Google tracking is unacceptable — use Brave Search instead
Full-page content extraction — Serper returns SERP metadata and snippets, not full scraped page content
Semantic or neural search over a custom corpus — this is a Google proxy, not a vector search engine
best_when: An agent needs the breadth and freshness of Google's search index with structured JSON output and near-instant latency (~200ms).
avoid_when: You need full page content extraction, privacy guarantees, or an independent non-Google index.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as X-API-KEY header. No scopes or per-endpoint key restrictions. Key management is done via the Serper dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 2,500 free queries on signup (one-time, not monthly)
pricing.paid_tiers: Credit packs starting at $50 for 50,000 queries (~$0.001/query); volume discounts available for higher tiers
pricing.notes: One of the lowest per-query prices in the category. Free credits are a one-time allocation, not a recurring monthly grant — agents in production will need a paid plan quickly.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; no hard published per-second limit for standard plans; burst-friendly for typical agent workloads

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies; 429 rate limit responses include Retry-After hints in some cases. Error messages are generally actionable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search is a read-only POST endpoint — identical queries return identical results (subject to Google freshness). Safe to retry on 5xx errors.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier is a one-time credit grant, not monthly — agents in production will exhaust free credits quickly and must be provisioned with a paid plan before hitting the quota wall
Results reflect Google's index and ranking, which can vary by apparent geography and time; agents must not assume deterministic result ordering across identical queries
Snippets are truncated Google-provided excerpts, not full page content — agents needing full text must follow up with a scraping API like Firecrawl or Jina
The API proxies Google's results and is subject to Google's search quality changes; result structure (presence of knowledge graph, featured snippet, etc.) is not guaranteed for every query
No semantic or intent-based query understanding — keyword construction matters and agents should use explicit search operators (site:, filetype:, etc.) for precise retrieval

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope restrictions; any key compromise grants full API access. No IP allowlisting or key expiry documented. Underlying Google results mean query data passes through Google infrastructure.
